title: Issue: undefined method 'dom_id' error when QuerySales model tool executes successfully
author: RayNawara
body:
Bug Report Details for Raif Gem

  Issue: undefined method 'dom_id' error when QuerySales model tool executes successfully

  Error Location:
  - Method: Raif::ConversationEntry#create_entry_for_observation!
  - File: /home/ray/.rbenv/versions/3.2.2/lib/ruby/gems/3.2.0/gems/raif-1.2.2/app/models/raif/conversation_entry.rb:86

  Full Error Message:
  undefined method `dom_id' for #<Raif::ConversationEntry model_response_message: nil, raw_response: nil, user_message: "Show me sales summary
   for XXX Ford this month", raif_conversation_id: 1, creator_id: 1, creator_type: "User", id: 7, started_at: "2025-08-07
  15:39:54.967952000 +0000", completed_at: "2025-08-07 15:39:56.512403000 +0000", failed_at: nil, created_at: "2025-08-07 15:39:54.949846000
  +0000", updated_at: "2025-08-07 15:39:56.512607000 +0000">

  Context:
  - Raif version: 1.2.2
  - Rails version: 8.0.2
  - Ruby version: 3.2.2
  - The model tool executes successfully and returns correct results
  - The error occurs when Raif tries to create an observation entry after tool execution
  - This happens in the background job context (Raif::ConversationEntryJob)

  Issue Description:
  The dom_id method is a Rails view helper that isn't available in ActiveJob/background job contexts. When a model tool completes successfully
   and Raif tries to create the observation entry, it calls dom_id which fails because view helpers aren't loaded in the job context.

  Steps to Reproduce:
  1. Create a custom model tool that successfully executes and returns results
  2. Set triggers_observation_to_model? to true
  3. Use the tool in a Raif conversation
  4. The tool executes successfully but fails when creating the observation entry

  Expected Behavior:
  The tool should execute and create the observation entry without errors.

  Actual Behavior:
  Tool executes successfully but fails with undefined method 'dom_id' error when creating observation entry, resulting in "There was an error
  generating this response" message to user.

title: Missing spec directory in exported gem
author: rowanhogan
body:
Hi 👋

I was just trying out the gem, and I got the following error when trying to run my application's test suite with rspec:

```
LoadError:
  cannot load such file -- [PATH_TO_APP]/.bundle/[BUNDLE_VERSION]/ruby/3.4.0/gems/raif-1.2.0/spec/support/test_llm
```

Is it perhaps because the `spec` directory is not included in the exported gemfile?

https://github.com/CultivateLabs/raif/blob/2b76741abffdde6aa9bf55f7f75724ecf7d47699/raif.gemspec#L21

Am I missing some other required setup step?

title: Setting Timeouts
author: thedumbtechguy
body:
Hi. Thanks for this Framework. Really well designed and fits our use cases exactly.
One thing we found with other tools was that we needed to increase our timeout since our processing can take quite a while to complete.

I could not find anyway to configure timeouts. Is this possible? or do would you accept a contribution?
If the latter, happy to do so according to your specs.

title: Streaming LLM Responses
author: dhanson358
body:
Really appreciate the work on this project, this is very well done and covers all the basics very nicely.  

I was wondering if you had given any thought to streaming responses from the LLM to provide a similar experience to many of the other desktop AI clients.  I'd be happy to try and chip in on making this work, but figured it would be worth a discussion if some work was already underway in this fashion.  

I think it should be as simple as creating a streaming interface on the LLM classes, then updating/re-broadcasting the conversation entry on each update?

title: Make AWS Bedrock integration more generic like OpenRouter
author: uxxman
body:
First of all great work 🥇 

AWS is now a more generic framework like OpenRouter with a unified API for accessing a wide range of LLM models but its is currently only tied to Anthropic Claude. 

The idea is make AWS bedrock integration more generic so we can easily add new models available via bedrock, especially the new efficient and low cost Amazon Nova models.

Will be you open for such a PR? We currently use a local fork for it.

title: Is mysql dependency necessary?
author: egilburg
body: I don't have mysql installed (only psql), but commenting it out of the Gemfile, makes the tests all pass with psql alone. If the gem logic is not db-specific, could we get rid of it as unnecessary (and heavy) dependency?

title: Support for File Inputs in Tasks
author: mauriciomenegaz
body:
First of all, thanks for the amazing work on Raif — it's been a joy to explore!

I'm trying to implement a use case that involves passing a file (e.g. an image for GPT-4 Vision, or a PDF for Claude) as input to a task. However, I couldn't find any examples of this in the README, and after digging through the source code, I couldn't identify a supported way to do it.

Specifically, since the _build_prompt_ method must return a string and there doesn't seem to be a lifecycle hook that allows injecting additional user messages before execution, I'm not sure how to include file inputs in a task.

Is this use case currently supported? Or would it require changes to the library?

I'd be happy to explore a PR to add support for this if it's out of scope today — just wanted to confirm I'm not overlooking an existing way to do it.
